name: Grape Seed Extract
historicalUse: Grape preparations appear throughout ancient Mediterranean and Arabic medical traditions. Rambam dedicated substantial attention to grapes and grape products in his dietary writings, noting their effects on cardiac vitality, blood quality, and digestive function. Ibn Sina and Dioscorides both described medicinal applications of grape seeds, skins, and leaves. The French paradox — low cardiovascular disease rates in populations consuming polyphenol-rich red wine — renewed modern scientific interest in grapevine constituents.
modernEvidence: Grape seed extract (GSE) standardized to 95% OPCs contains procyanidin oligomers and monomers (catechin, epicatechin). Multiple RCTs demonstrate significant improvements in endothelial function, reduction of LDL oxidation, lowering of systolic blood pressure, and anti-edema effects. OPCs have among the highest measured antioxidant capacity (ORAC) of any dietary phenolic compounds.
mechanisms: Endothelial protection — OPCs bind collagen and elastin, protecting vascular matrix integrity
LDL antioxidation — prevents copper-catalyzed LDL oxidation, reducing atherogenic potential
ACE inhibition — reduces vasoconstriction via angiotensin II reduction
Platelet aggregation inhibition — reduces thrombotic risk at vascular injury sites
NF-kB inhibition — reduces vascular inflammatory signaling
benefits: Supports healthy blood pressure already within normal range
Supports vascular integrity and endothelial function
Contributes to leg circulation comfort and reduction of edema
safetyProfile: Excellent safety profile. Well tolerated at 100–300mg/day. No significant adverse events in clinical trials.
contraindications: Caution pre-surgery due to antiplatelet activity
Pregnancy — insufficient data at therapeutic doses
drugInteractions: Anticoagulants — additive antiplatelet effect; monitor INR
Iron supplements — OPCs may reduce iron absorption; separate doses by 2+ hours
physicianNotes: Appropriate in cardiovascular risk reduction protocols, chronic venous insufficiency, and as antioxidant support. Additive benefit with CoQ10 and hawthorn in circulatory support protocols. Monitor anticoagulation in patients on warfarin.
patientGuidance: If you take blood thinners or iron supplements, inform your physician and separate iron supplement doses by at least 2 hours.
